question: Among DC wards 7 and 8, identify which ward had the higher average 311 service requests from 2019 through 2020. Who represents that ward on the DC Council? This council member earned a graduate degree from a university whose flagship building is a National Historic Landmark. Among the founder of the university and the person the flagship building is named after, in what year was the earlier-born individual born? Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
strengths: The question cleanly motivates the full comparison-to-biographical chain without leaking the final target.
The task stays comfortably within budget while still requiring multiple meaningful hops.
concerns: The councilmember hop is mildly snapshot-sensitive.
The flagship-building landmark qualifier is implied more than explicitly used in the decomposition.
rationale: This artifact is internally coherent and the decomposition follows naturally from the final question: compare the two wards across 2019-2020, identify the relevant councilmember, trace the graduate university, then compare the founder and building namesake by birth year. The seven-dataset footprint is comfortably affordable for a core slice, and the final hop remains attached to the question rather than feeling decorative. The only mild roughness is temporal sensitivity around the present-tense councilmember lookup and the fact that the National Historic Landmark descriptor is not doing much operational work in the chain, but neither issue materially harms derivability or benchmark suitability.
suggested_fix: Anchor the councilmember step to the dataset snapshot and make the flagship-building/National Historic Landmark link explicit in one node.
